claims.text: Foundation models based on prior-data fitted networks (PFNs) have shown strong empirical performance in causal inference.
claims.source_quote: Foundation models based on prior-data fitted networks (PFNs) have shown strong empirical performance in causal inference by framing the task as an in-context learning problem.

claims.text: PFNs frame causal inference as an in-context learning problem.
claims.source_quote: Foundation models based on prior-data fitted networks (PFNs) have shown strong empirical performance in causal inference by framing the task as an in-context learning problem.

claims.text: It is unclear whether PFN-based causal estimators provide uncertainty quantification that is consistent with classical frequentist estimators.
claims.source_quote: However, it is unclear whether PFN-based causal estimators provide uncertainty quantification that is consistent with classical frequentist estimators.

claims.text: Existing PFNs, when interpreted as Bayesian ATE estimators, can exhibit prior-induced confounding bias.
claims.source_quote: We show that existing PFNs, when interpreted as Bayesian ATE estimators, can exhibit prior-induced confounding bias

claims.text: In existing PFNs interpreted as Bayesian ATE estimators, the prior is not asymptotically overwritten by data.
claims.source_quote: the prior is not asymptotically overwritten by data, which, in turn, prevents frequentist consistency.

claims.text: The prior not being asymptotically overwritten by data prevents frequentist consistency in existing PFN-based ATE estimators.
claims.source_quote: the prior is not asymptotically overwritten by data, which, in turn, prevents frequentist consistency.

claims.text: A calibration procedure based on a one-step posterior correction (OSPC) can help restore frequentist consistency for PFN-based ATE estimators.
claims.source_quote: As a remedy, we suggest employing a calibration procedure based on a one-step posterior correction (OSPC). We show that the OSPC helps to restore frequentist consistency

claims.text: The OSPC can yield a semi-parametric Bernstein-von Mises theorem for calibrated PFNs.
claims.source_quote: We show that the OSPC helps to restore frequentist consistency and can yield a semi-parametric Bernstein-von Mises theorem for calibrated PFNs

claims.text: Both calibrated PFN-based estimators and classical semi-parametric efficient estimators converge in distribution with growing data size under the semi-parametric Bernstein-von Mises theorem.
claims.source_quote: both the calibrated PFN-based estimators and the classical semi-parametric efficient estimators converge in distribution with growing data size

claims.text: OSPC is implemented through tailoring martingale posteriors on top of the PFNs.
claims.source_quote: we implement OSPC through tailoring martingale posteriors on top of the PFNs.

claims.text: Martingale posteriors on top of PFNs allow recovery of functional nuisance posteriors required by the OSPC.
claims.source_quote: In this way, we are able to recover functional nuisance posteriors from PFNs, required by the OSPC.

claims.text: In multiple semi-synthetic experiments, PFNs calibrated with martingale posterior OSPC produce ATE uncertainty that asymptotically matches frequentist uncertainty.
claims.source_quote: In multiple (semi-)synthetic experiments, PFNs calibrated with our martingale posterior OSPC produce ATE uncertainty that (i) asymptotically matches frequentist uncertainty

claims.text: In multiple semi-synthetic experiments, PFNs calibrated with martingale posterior OSPC produce ATE uncertainty that is well calibrated in finite samples compared to other Bayesian ATE estimators.
claims.source_quote: PFNs calibrated with our martingale posterior OSPC produce ATE uncertainty that ... (ii) is well calibrated in finite samples in comparison to other Bayesian ATE estimators.
